summary: Gruber reviews the 2017 iPad Pro models, finding them exceptional across every dimension — display, Pencil latency, size, battery, and performance — with no tradeoffs or downsides. The 120 Hz ProMotion display and dramatically reduced Pencil latency are standout improvements that must be experienced in person. He highlights the jaw-dropping CPU performance, noting the iPad Pro nearly matches MacBook Pro single-core scores and doubles the one-port MacBook in multi-core. However, he argues the real story is iOS 11, which he believes is the reason Apple waited until WWDC to announce the hardware. After installing the iOS 11 beta, he describes the experience as transformative, feeling like a hand untied from behind his back. He frames Apple's chip team as vastly underappreciated and pulling ahead of the entire industry.
key_points: The 2017 iPad Pro has no tradeoffs — every dimension (display, Pencil, size, performance, battery) is improved with nothing given up
The A10X chip nearly matches MacBook Pro single-core performance and doubles the one-port MacBook's multi-core score, putting Apple in an awkward position relative to Intel
The 10.5-inch size is a perfect upgrade from 9.7 inches, improving keyboard usability with no perceptible increase in bulk
iOS 11 is the real iPad story — the hardware review is almost secondary to what the new software enables for productivity
Apple's in-house chip team is pulling ahead of the industry in ways that are vastly underappreciated
money_quotes: This is, in many ways, the easiest product review I've ever written. There are several significant improvements to the hardware, and no tradeoffs or downsides. There is no 'but'.
You really do have to see the 120 Hz refresh rate in person — and play with it while scrolling content on screen — to get it. You can actually read text as it's moving during a scroll.
The latency of the Apple Pencil on the new iPad Pro is so much better — so much closer to ink-on-paper imperceptibility — that you have to try it to believe it.
That sort of year-over-year increase just doesn't happen anymore, but here we are.
I think it is vastly underappreciated just how significantly Apple's chip team is pulling ahead of the industry, especially Intel.
Apple's A10X chip is so high-performing that I think it's put Apple in a slightly uncomfortable position marketing-wise. They can't brag about it fully without making Intel look bad.
It feels like a hand has been untied from behind my back, and this amazing hardware has finally been allowed to run free.
key_insight: The 2017 iPad Pro hardware is flawless and its chip performance is embarrassing Intel, but the real transformation is iOS 11 finally letting the hardware reach its potential.
